response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack/web software developer with strong TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang backgrounds, but the role requires deep hardware expertise — SoC/IP modeling, AI-accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in the resume. His low-latency systems work at Dunnhumby and brief C/C++ experience at National Instruments are the closest matches, but they fall far short of principal-level silicon performance architecture. He meets the years-of-experience bar with a BS, yet the domain mismatch is fundamental.
strengths: Long, continuous engineering career (15+ years) satisfying BS + 8 years requirement
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching in DLang)
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship, and teaching
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon design, or hardware performance-architecture experience
No AI/deep-learning workload, GPU, SIMD/MIMD, or accelerator optimization background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, not principal-level hardware systems architecture

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or DNN workload experience
